text:
Violence against health workers is emerging as a new global public health problem that affects the physical and mental health of health workers and, most importantly, threatens the quality of health care provided to patients. According to the World Health Organization (WHO), violence and abuse in the workplace is an emerging global public health problem that undermines the health and wellbeing of health workers and compromises the quality of health care provided to patients. Workplace violence is becoming more frequent in our country and is being mentioned in the scientific literature and media in our country. This study provides a literature review about the violence against health workers in the light of the relevant literature, their causes, such as lack of functionality in the health system, communication errors, heavy workload and forms such as language, physical or psychological violence.

Our analysis relies on the official statistical data about violence against health workers available in Türkiye and also in selected countries in the world such as USA, Australia and several countries from Europe and Africa where physician and nurse violence especially in the emergency department are reported to be the highest. Besides, we analyzed legal rights and freedoms of health workers under the Turkish Penal Code and the Turkish Code of Obligations related to workplaces and accordingly investigated the recent legislation imposed by the “White Code” and the increase of judicial penalties aimed at eliminating the violence against health workers. In the light of our findings, the ways and means for solving this humanitarian problem arising from an increasing violence are recommended consisting mainly of workplace safety measures, humane working conditions and governmental interventions for the defense of the health workers, in whose hands the society entrusts the critical task of providing health security.
